title: wide interface — 30 mixed-type properties
description: Single interface with 30+ properties spanning scalars, Date, bigint, nested object — exercises the per-field walk cost without any union dispatch.
notes: The Date fields (`createdAt`/`updatedAt` and nested `meta.lastSeen`) JSON-encode to ISO strings and revive to Dates; the `big1`/`big2` bigints encode to decimal strings (rebuilt via `BigInt(...)`) on the JSON wire and take the binary string-fallback path.
schema:
JsonEncoder(
  RT.object({
    id: RT.number(),
    name: RT.string(),
    description: RT.string(),
    createdAt: RT.date(),
    updatedAt: RT.date(),
    isActive: RT.boolean(),
    score: RT.number(),
    rank: RT.number(),
    tag1: RT.string(),
    tag2: RT.string(),
    tag3: RT.string(),
    tag4: RT.string(),
    tag5: RT.string(),
    count1: RT.number(),
    count2: RT.number(),
    count3: RT.number(),
    flag1: RT.boolean(),
    flag2: RT.boolean(),
    flag3: RT.boolean(),
    big1: RT.bigint(),
    big2: RT.bigint(),
    alias: RT.string(),
    email: RT.string(),
    city: RT.string(),
    country: RT.string(),
    postal: RT.string(),
    width: RT.number(),
    height: RT.number(),
    weight: RT.number(),
    meta: RT.object({category: RT.string(), priority: RT.number(), lastSeen: RT.date()}),
  })
)
